intro: Werauhia leucophylla, also known as Dracaena leucophylla, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and lower blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Werauhia leucophylla include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
